Article.ArticleTitle: Are we heading towards a cybersecurity crisis in health care and are actions needed?

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Else Kröner Fresenius Center for Digital Health, TUD Dresden University of Technology, Dresden, Germany.
Institute of Global Health Innovation, Imperial College London, London W2 1NY, UK. Electronic address: k.rajput24@imperial.ac.uk.
Else Kröner Fresenius Center for Digital Health, TUD Dresden University of Technology, Dresden, Germany.
Else Kröner Fresenius Center for Digital Health, TUD Dresden University of Technology, Dresden, Germany.
Institute of Global Health Innovation, Imperial College London, London W2 1NY, UK.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: OF and KR are joint first authors. OF has a leadership role and holds stock in WhalesDontFly GmbH and has had consulting relationships with Prova Health. StG declares a non-financial interest (no consulting fees received) as an Advisory Group member of the Ernst & Young-coordinated “Study on Regulatory Governance and Innovation in the field of Medical Devices” conducted on behalf of the DG SANTE of the European Commission. Further, StG declares the following competing financial interests: he has or has had consulting relationships with Una Health GmbH, Lindus Health, Flo, ICURA ApS, Rock Health, Thymia, FORUM Institut für Management GmbH, High-Tech Gründerfonds Management GmbH, DG Research and Innovation, Prova Health, Haleon plc, and Ada Health GmbH; and holds share options in Ada Health GmbH. KR, MO, and SaG declare no competing interests. All authors were involved in conceptualisation, validation, and visualisation of the Comment. StG and SaG supervised the study. OF and KR wrote the original draft of the manuscript. MO, StG, and SaG were involved in reviewing and editing the manuscript. This work was supported by the European Commission under the Horizon Europe programme, as part of the projects CYMEDSEC (101094218) and ASSESS-DHT (101137347). The views and opinions expressed are those of the authors only and do not necessarily reflect those of the EU. Neither the EU nor the granting authorities can be held responsible for them. Responsibility for the information and views expressed therein lies entirely with the authors. Further, this work was supported by the German Federal Ministry of Research, Technology and Space (BMFTR) as part of the Zunkuftscluster SEMECO (03ZU1210BA) and supported by BMFTR through the EU-financed NextGenerationEU programme under project PATH (Personal Mastery of Health and Wellness Data; grant number 16KISA100K). During the preparation of this work, the authors used DeepL, Grammarly, and ChatGPT (GPT-3.5, GPT-4, and GPT-4o) to improve the grammar, spelling, and readability of the manuscript. After using these tools and services, the authors reviewed and edited the content as needed and take full responsibility for the content of the publication.
